_comment: Single source of truth for the Bookie setup steps. SETUP.md and the emailed checklist render FROM this file so they can't drift. Each step has an owner: 'claude' (I do it), 'john' (only he can — auth/MFA/legal/account-creation/physical), or 'deferred' (not needed for first light). blocked_on lists step ids that must be done first. The harness goal verify turns RED if any claude-owned, unblocked step is left incomplete — which structurally forces me to do my own work instead of leaving it assigned to John.
title: Bookie QBO sandbox setup
critical_path_note: First light (Bookie reading your books) needs only steps 4 and 5 from John. Steps 9-11 are mine. Steps 6-8 are deferred (browser-automation surface, a later phase).

steps.title: Intuit Developer account
steps.detail: Signed up at https://developer.intuit.com using john@husband.llc. Builder tier, free.

steps.title: Workspace + Bookie app
steps.why_owner: Done inside John's authenticated Intuit dashboard
steps.detail: Workspace created in My Hub > Workspaces; app of type 'QuickBooks Online (Accounting)' named Bookie. AppID bb87a65f...

steps.title: Development keys saved to laptop
steps.why_owner: Keys are read from John's authenticated dashboard
steps.detail: Client ID + Secret from Keys & Credentials > Development saved to /home/john/.config/bookie/qbo-credentials.json (0600, gitignored).

steps.title: Fill Intuit Settings tabs, save Redirect URI
steps.why_owner: Requires John logged into Intuit's dashboard; step 4c (accept legal Terms of Service) must be John's explicit act
steps.detail: Fill Basic app info, App URLs (placeholders ok for sandbox), accept Terms of Service, pick Accounting category, set Geolocation=US. Then Redirect URIs unlocks: add http://localhost:8910/qbo-callback (Development pill, no trailing slash). Add same as Reconnect URL.

steps.title: Run OAuth handshake
steps.claude_assist: I can launch the script; the in-browser Intuit sign-in + Authorize click is John's (auth + consent)
steps.why_owner: OAuth consent to grant the app access to QBO data must be John's click, behind his Intuit login + MFA
steps.detail: Run: bash /home/john/repos/Bookie/scripts/qbo-authorize.sh — browser opens, sign in as john@husband.llc, choose the sandbox company, click Authorize. Script writes refresh_token + realm_id to the credentials file.

steps.title: Create Bookie bot Intuit user
steps.why_owner: Browser-automation surface only; not needed for API-based first light
steps.detail: Deferred. Needed later for the QBO web-UI automation surface (For Review queue, Bank Rules). Requires account creation + email verification + MFA — John's when we get there.

steps.title: Capture bot browser session (one-time MFA)
steps.why_owner: MFA challenge to John's phone; browser-automation surface only

steps.title: Sanity-check the laptop
steps.why_owner: Pure commands — ls + bookie self-check. Mine.

steps.title: Watch Bookie's first inspection tick
steps.why_owner: Pure commands — pull + read inbox. Mine.
steps.detail: I run the pull and read inbox/bookie.md to confirm the first-inspection summary landed.
